reasoning: DRAM Bandwidth Utilization is 111.4% of peak, indicating the kernel is fully saturating memory bandwidth. The arithmetic intensity is 0.00 FLOP/byte, far below the ridge point of 62 FLOP/byte, confirming the kernel performs negligible computation relative to its memory traffic. While Long Scoreboard stalls are 97.5%, this is consistent with warps waiting on memory requests when DRAM is saturated — the high DRAM utilization (>50%) distinguishes this as a true memory-bandwidth bottleneck rather than a latency-bound scenario.
